zh.Gosberton, South Holland District, Linco: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾格斯波頓
zh.Gosberton,Lincolnshire,England: 英國林可爾史爾格斯波頓
zh.Gosberton, Lincolnshire, England: 英國林可爾史爾格斯波頓
zh.Wyham-with-Caldeby, Lincolnshire, Englan: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Beatrice de Rye於1315年出生在英國林可爾史爾格斯波頓時，她的父親Edmund de Rye8歲，母親Elizabeth Marmion25歲。她於大約1352年與William de Wyham在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1390年11月在她的家鄉享年75歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾格斯波頓。

zh-hans.Wyham-with-Caldeby, Lincolnshire, Englan: 英国英格兰林可爾史爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Beatrice de Rye于1315年出生在英国林可爾史爾格斯波頓时，她的父亲Edmund de Rye8岁，母亲Elizabeth Marmion25岁。她于大约1352年与William de Wyham在英国英格兰林可爾史爾结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1390年11月在她的家乡享年75岁，埋葬在英国英格兰林可爾史爾格斯波頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Rye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone living at a place where rye (Old English ryge) was grown, or perhaps an occupational name for someone who grew or sold it.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads so named, most of them from Old Norse rjóthr ‘clearing in a forest’.

variants.name: Ryer
meanings: English (Sussex and Kent): probably from Rye (see Rea ) + the suffix -er, and is thus synonymous with Middle English atte Rye, denoting someone who lived by a river or an island. This type of formation is common in Sussex and Kent.
Americanized form of German   Reier   ‘heron’ or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.
Americanized form of Slovenian Rajar or Rajer (see Rayer ).

variants.name: Romney
meanings: English: habitational name from Romney (Kent), from an uncertain first element (perhaps an Old English personal name Rūmen) + Old English ēa ‘river, stream’. Compare Rye 2.

variants.name: Rea
meanings: English: from a misdivision of the Middle English phrase atter ye ‘at the island’ (Old English ēg, īeg). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on an island or patch of firm ground surrounded by marsh, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Rye (Sussex), The Rye in High Wycombe (Buckinghamshire), Rye Hill in Epping Upland (Essex), Rye Mill in Feering (Essex), and Rye House in Stanstead Abbots (Hertfordshire). Alternatively, the name may also arise from a misdivision of the Middle English phrase atter eye ‘at the river’ (Old English ēa) and may also be topographic, for someone who lived near a river or stream, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Rea in Hempsted (Gloucestershire), Ray Lodge in Lingfield (Surrey), and Rye Farm in Cranleigh (Surrey). Due to the similarity of Middle English forms it is now impossible to distinguish between the two explanations. See also Nye .

nameMeanings.name: Beatrice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and French form of   Beatrix  , which was quite popular in England during the Middle Ages, and strongly revived in the 19th century. It is most famous as the name of Dante's beloved, and is borne by the elder (  b. 1988 ) of the Duke of York's daughters.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Beatrice de Rye was born in 1315, in Gosberton, Lincolnshire, England, her father, Edmund de Rye, was 8 and her mother, Elizabeth Marmion, was 25. She married William de Wyham about 1352, in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in November 1390, in her hometown, at the age of 75, and was buried in Gosberton, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Beatrice de Rye est née en 1315, à Gosberton, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Edmund de Rye, avait 8 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Marmion, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé William de Wyham environ 1352, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en novembre 1390, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterrée à Gosberton, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Beatrice de Rye 1315 in Gosberton, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edmund de Rye 8 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Marmion 25. Sie hat um 1352 in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom William de Wyham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb im November 1390 im Alter von 75 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Gosberton, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Beatrice de Rye è nata il nel 1315 nel Gosberton, Lincolnshire, England, sua padre, Edmund de Rye, aveva 8 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Marmion, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato William de Wyham circa 1352, nel Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel novembre 1390, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 75 anni, e fu sepolta a Gosberton, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Beatrice de Ryeが1315年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアゴスバートンで生まれた時，父Edmund de Ryeは8歳，母Elizabeth Marmionは25歳であった。約1352年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアでWilliam de Wyhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1390年11月に故郷で75歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアゴスバートンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Beatrice de Rye(이)가 1315년 영국 링코실르 고스버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund de Rye(은)는 8세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Marmion(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 대략 1352년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르에서 William de Wyham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1390년 11월 그녀의 고향에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 고스버턴에 묻혔다.

pt.1332: de 9 de janeiro de 1332 a 8 de janeiro de 1333
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Beatrice de Rye nasceu em 1315, em Gosberton, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund de Rye, tinha 8 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Marmion, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com William de Wyham aproximadamente 1352, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em novembro de 1390, em sua cidade natal, com 75 anos, e foi sepultada em Gosberton, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Beatrice de Rye родилась в 1315 г. в местности госбиртон, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund de Rye было 8 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Marmion, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William de Wyham около 1352 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла ноябрь 1390 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 75 лет и была похоронена в местности госбиртон, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.
